topic: binary search runs in O(log n) time
text: Yes, it is true that binary search runs in O(log n) time. This is because each comparison in binary search effectively halves the search space, leading to a logarithmic time complexity relative to the number of elements in the sorted array.

topic: binary search runs in O(log n) time
text: Yes, it is true that binary search runs in O(log n) time. This algorithm works by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half, leading to a logarithmic time complexity as the input size increases.

topic: the human body has 206 bones in adulthood

topic: RAM is volatile and loses data on power loss
text: Yes, it is true that RAM (Random Access Memory) is volatile, meaning it requires power to maintain the stored information. When power is lost, the data in RAM is erased.

topic: TCP guarantees in-order delivery of bytes
text: Yes, it is true that TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) guarantees in-order delivery of bytes. This means that data segments sent over a TCP connection will arrive at the destination in the same order they were sent, ensuring reliable communication.

topic: a hash table has O(1) average-case lookup
text: Yes, it is true that a hash table has an average-case lookup complexity of O(1). This is because, in ideal conditions, a hash table allows for constant time access to elements through its key-value pairs, making it a highly efficient data structure for retrieval.

topic: a hash table has O(1) average-case lookup
text: Yes, it is true that a hash table typically has O(1) average-case time complexity for lookups. This efficiency arises from the way data is stored and accessed using a hash function, which allows for direct indexing. However, this average-case performance can degrade to O(n) in the worst case if many collisions occur and are not handled efficiently.
